plan.description:
This project proposal aims to investigate how digital transformation affects corporate identity management (CIM) in the digital age. There are still many uncertainties how organisations can effectively manage their corporate identity in the context of digital transformation, and it needs to be clarified how organisations can effectively measure the impact of their CIM efforts in the digital environment.
Corporate Identity in the Digital Age: the Impact of Digital Transformation on Corporate Identity Management and Strategy formation (Nr. LU-BA-PA-2024/1-0023)
plan.title: Corporate Identity in the Digital Age: the Impact of Digital Transformation on Corporate Identity Management and Strategy formation

systemField.label: Description
systemField.description: Briefly describe the context and purpose of the DMP

systemField.label: Language
systemField.description: Select the language of your DMP

referenceField.label: Researchers
referenceField.description: Add here the names of people that have produced, processed, analysed the data described in the DMP.

referenceField.label: Organizations
referenceField.description: Add here the names of the organizations contributing to the creation and revision of the DMPs

referenceField.label: Funding organizations
referenceField.description: Select a funder of your research or add new

referenceField.label: Grants
referenceField.description: Find the grant of your research or add new

referenceField.label: Project
referenceField.description: Projects in Argos are perceived as distinct activities falling under a grant or common activities under different grants in collaborative schemas, eg open call for contributions. Please complete it for the grant associated to your organization if your project falls under this category. In all other cases, please leave blank and it will be autocompleted.

systemField.label: Access Rights
systemField.description: Choose how the DMP is displayed after is published on Zenodo. By choosing Open Access, the DMP will be open on Zenodo after the Publication Date. By choosing Restricted Access, the DMP will be restricted after the publication is made.

referenceField.label: License
referenceField.description: Assign a license to your DMP by selecting the most appropriate from the list.

reference.label: BA School of Business and Finance

description.description: The Data Management Plan (DMP) is developed in the framework of the Recovery and Resilience Facility project "Internal and External Consolidation of the University of Latvia" (No.5.2.1.1.i.0/2/24/I/CFLA/007), grant project “Corporate Identity in the Digital Age: the Impact of Digital Transformation on Corporate Identity Management and Strategy formation”, No. LU-BA-PA-2024/1-0023)
description.label: Banku augstskola

descriptionTemplate.label: Science Europe
descriptionTemplate.description: This is the Science Europe template based on the RDM Practical Guide (https://www.scienceeurope.org/media/jezkhnoo/se_rdm_practical_guide_final.pdf)

page.title: Data Description And Collection Or Re-use Of Existing Data

section.title: Data Description And Collection Or Re-use Of Existing Data

referenceTypeData.label: Select a service / tool from the list

labelData.label: Couldn't find it? Insert it manually

fieldSet.title: Explain which methodologies or software will be used if new data are collected or produced

labelData.label: Please provide URL/Location of the DMP where reused data are documented
Couldn't find the data repository? Insert it manually
Couldn't find the dataset? Insert it manually

fieldSet.title: Are there any constraints on the re-use of existing data?
fieldSet.description: Apart from data produced within your research, you might want to repurpose data that have been produced and shared by others, in different research context.
fieldSet.extendedDescription: If you are planning to use other researchers’ data in your research there are a couple of things that you need to take into consideration, such as copyright of datasets, permissions provided through licenses, ethical aspects for reuse, etc. Learn more: https://www.openaire.eu/can-i-reuse-someone-else-research-data

selectData.label: What is the origin of the data?

labelData.label: How do you keep track of data history?

fieldSet.title: Explain how data provenance will be documented
fieldSet.description: Briefly state what is the data origin and how it will be tracked so that history of data is available and recorded.

fieldSet.title: Briefly state the reasons if the re-use of any existing data sources has been considered but discarded

section.title: How will new data be collected or produced and/or how will existing data be re-used?

fieldSet.title: Give details on the data types
fieldSet.description: The main distinction between data types is between primary data and secondary data. Data collection may contain both primary and secondary data depending on the source where they have been derived from. Primary data is data that have been collected for the first time and have not undergone through data processing and/or analysis, yet. Secondary data is data that have been cleaned up, analysed and shared by others (published or unpublished) and they are those that are being typically reused. In addition, types of data unveil how they have been collected so they also depend on the process that led to their generation.
fieldSet.extendedDescription: For more information, have a look at: https://data.library.virginia.edu/data-management/plan/format-types/

options.label: Text files - MS Word docs, .txt files, PDF, RTF, XML (Extensible Markup Language)
options.value: Text files - MS Word docs, .txt files, PDF, RTF, XML (Extensible Markup Language)

options.label: Text files - MS Word docs, .txt files, PDF, RTF, XML (Extensible Markup Language)
options.value: Text files - MS Word docs, .txt files, PDF, RTF, XML (Extensible Markup Language)

options.label: Multimedia - jpg / jpeg, gif, tiff, png, mpeg, mp4, QuickTime
options.value: Multimedia - jpg / jpeg, gif, tiff, png, mpeg, mp4, QuickTime

options.label: Instrument specific formats - Olympus Confocal Microscope Data Format, Carl Zeiss
options.value: Instrument specific formats - Olympus Confocal Microscope Data Format, Carl Zeiss

fieldSet.title: Give details on the data format
fieldSet.description: Data may be found in different formats. Usually, outputs of specific instruments/ services/ tools where data are generated and/ or processed have a default way of exposing their outputs following specific standards. Most common distinctions between formats are proprietary and non proprietary formats, because they may impact data access in the long-term.
fieldSet.extendedDescription: For more information about formats, have a look at: https://www.ukdataservice.ac.uk/manage-data/format/recommended-formats

labelData.label: Will you use standardised formats for your data?
Couldn't find it? Insert it manually
Please describe the formats you plan to store your data in, including any URLs to documentation
Are the file formats you will use open?
Please describe which data are not in an open format and why

fieldSet.title: Justify the use of certain formats
fieldSet.description: For example, decisions may be based on staff expertise within the host organisation, a preference for open formats, standards accepted by data repositories, widespread usage within the research community, or on the software or equipment that will be used.
fieldSet.extendedDescription: Give preference to open and standard formats as they facilitate sharing and long-term re-use of data (several repositories provide lists of such ‘preferred formats’).

fieldSet.title: Give details on the volumes
fieldSet.description: They can be expressed in storage space required (bytes), and/or in numbers of objects, files, rows, and columns.

section.title: What data (for example the kind, formats, and volumes), will be collected or produced?
Documentation And Data Quality

fieldSet.title: Indicate which metadata will be provided to help others identify and discover the data
fieldSet.description: Metadata is data about data and is an essential set of information describing scientific outputs, in the form of either physical or digital objects, in a machine-readable format. According to the expected use, metadata can be given different attributes. Most common type which enables discovery and identification are descriptive metadata. Descriptive metadata contain information about key aspects needed to search for and successfully find a given scientific output, e.g. by its title, author/creator, abstract, keywords. Moreover, metadata may be used for describing a service or a scientific instrument.

referenceTypeData.label: Select a metadata standard from the list

labelData.label: Couldn't find it? Insert it manually

fieldSet.title: Indicate which metadata standards will be used
fieldSet.description: You may browse the Metadata Standards Catalog here: http://rd-alliance.github.io/metadata-directory/. Use community metadata standards where these are in place.

labelData.label: Provide more details and examples on used naming conversions

fieldSet.title: Indicate how the data will be organised during the project
fieldSet.description: Mentioning for example conventions, version control, and folder structures. Consistent, well-ordered research data will be easier to find, understand, and re-use.
fieldSet.extendedDescription: Naming conventions used throughout the management process, such as file names or data entities, should be clearly described in order to make data understandable by other researchers who may reuse them after the project ends. Check out some examples of file naming best practices: http://tiny.cc/op8lcz Versioning may be a built-in functionality, subject to an instrument/service/tool that has been used for data processing and analysis which automatically produces versioned data. In other occasions, you will need to keep track and adapt to a versioning mechanism for your data. Find out more about how to handle and arrange different versions: https://www.openaire.eu/raw-data-backup-and-versioning

fieldSet.title: Consider what other documentation is needed to enable re-use
fieldSet.description: This may include information on the methodology used to collect the data, analytical and procedural information, definitions of variables, units of measurement, and so on.

options.label: database with links to each item
options.value: database with links to each item

fieldSet.title: Consider how this information will be captured and where it will be recorded

section.description: For example the methodology of data collection and way of organizing data
section.title: What metadata and documentation will accompany the data?

fieldSet.title: Explain how the consistency and quality of data collection will be controlled and documented

section.title: What data quality control measures will be used?

page.title: Storage And Backup During The Research Process

section.title: Storage And Backup During The Research Process

referenceTypeData.label: Select from a service/ tool from the list

labelData.label: Couldn't find it? Insert it manually

fieldSet.title: Describe where the data will be stored and backed up during research activities and how often the backup will be performed
fieldSet.description: Give preference to the use of robust, managed storage with automatic backup, such as provided by IT support services of the home institution. It is recommended to store data in least at two separate locations.
fieldSet.extendedDescription: Storing data on laptops, stand-alone hard drives, or external storage devices such as USB sticks is not recommended. To learn more about best practices for back up and data security, you may have a look at: https://www.openaire.eu/raw-data-backup-and-versioning and https://www.uu.nl/en/research/research-data-management/guides/storing-and-preserving-data

section.title: How will data and metadata be stored and backed up during the research?

fieldSet.title: Explain how the data will be recovered in the event of an incident
Explain who will have access to the data during the research and how access to data is controlled, especially in collaborative partnerships

labelData.label: Please provide more information about sensitive data used
How are risks going to be managed?

fieldSet.title: Describe the main risks and how these will be managed
fieldSet.description: Consider data protection, particularly if your data is sensitive for example containing personal data, politically sensitive information, or trade secrets.

labelData.label: Provide more information and URL where policies can be found

fieldSet.title: Explain which institutional data protection policies are in place

section.title: How will data security and protection of sensitive data be taken care of during the research?

page.title: Legal And Ethical Requirements, Codes Of Conduct

section.title: Legal And Ethical Requirements, Codes Of Conduct

fieldSet.title: If personal data are processed, how will compliance with legislation on personal data and on security be ensured?
fieldSet.description: Ensure that when dealing with personal data data protection laws (for example GDPR) are complied with: ›› Gain informed consent for preservation and/or sharing of personal data. ›› Consider anonymisation of personal data for preservation and/or sharing (truly anonymous data are no longer considered personal data). ›› Consider pseudonymisation of personal data (the main difference with anonymisation is that pseudonymisation is reversible). ›› Consider encryption which is seen as a special case of pseudonymisation (the encryption key must be stored separately from the data, for instance by a trusted third party).

fieldSet.title: Explain whether there is a managed access procedure in place for authorised users of personal data

section.title: Personal data
How will other legal issues, such as intellectual property rights and ownership, be managed? What legislation is applicable?

labelData.label: Couldn't find it? Insert it manually

fieldSet.title: Who will be the owner of the data
fieldSet.description: Meaning who will have the rights to control access.

fieldSet.title: Explain what access conditions will apply to the data?
Will the data be openly accessible, or will there be access restrictions?
What kind of access restrictions?

section.description: Make sure to cover these matters of rights to control access to data for multi-partner projects and multiple data owners, in the consortium agreement.
section.title: Data ownership and accessibility

fieldSet.title: Are intellectual property rights affected?
fieldSet.extendedDescription: For example Database Directive, sui generis rights

fieldSet.title: Explain which intellectual property and how will they be dealt with

section.title: Intellectual property rights

fieldSet.title: Indicate whether there are any restrictions on the re-use of third-party data.

section.title: Third-party data restrictions

selectData.label: What methods are used to manage personal/ sensitive data?

options.label: Privacy constraints and applicable ethical norms
options.value: Privacy constraints and applicable ethical norms

fieldSet.title: What ethical issues and codes of conduct (CoC) are there, and how will they be taken into account?
fieldSet.description: It is possible that data are sensitive, such as personal or confidential, and hence they can not be shared directly to others. For those types of data, specific processes need to be made in order to ensure their reliable exchange in the short and long-term.
fieldSet.extendedDescription: Find out more about sensitive data and how to process them: https://www.openaire.eu/sensitive-data-guide

section.description: Consider whether ethical issues can affect how data are stored and transferred, who can see or use them, and how long they are kept. Demonstrate awareness of these aspects and respective planning. • Follow the national and international codes of conducts and institutional ethical guidelines, and check if ethical review (for example by an ethics committee) is required for data collection in the research project.
section.title: Ethical issues
Data Sharing And Long-term Preservation

selectData.label: How will the data be discoverable?

options.label: use of a secure data service
options.value: use of a secure data service

fieldSet.title: Explain how the data will be discoverable and shared
fieldSet.extendedDescription: For example by deposit in a trustworthy data repository, indexed in a catalogue, use of a secure data service, direct handling of data requests, or use of another mechanism.

labelData.label: How long will the data be retained?

fieldSet.title: Outline the plan for data preservation and give information on how long the data will be retained
Explain when the data will be made available
Indicate the expected timely release
Will exclusive use of the data be claimed?
For what reason and how long exclusive use will be claimed?
Indicate whether data sharing will be postponed or restricted
fieldSet.extendedDescription: For example to publish, protect intellectual property, or seek patents.

fieldSet.title: Indicate who will be able to use the data
Is it necessary to restrict access to certain communities or to apply a data sharing agreement?
Explain how and why access will be restricted to certain communities or data sharing agreements might apply
Explain what action will be taken to overcome or to minimise restrictions

section.title: How and when will data be shared? Are there possible restrictions to data sharing or embargo reasons?

labelData.label: Provide URL of data, if applicable

fieldSet.title: Indicate what data must be retained or destroyed for contractual, legal, or regulatory purposes.
Indicate how it will be decided what data to keep
Describe the data to be preserved long-term
Explain the foreseeable research uses (and/ or users) for the data.

labelData.label: Couldn't find it? Insert it manually

fieldSet.title: Indicate where the data will be deposited
Will there be established repository proposed?
Demonstrate that the data can be curated effectively beyond the lifetime of the grant
fieldSet.description: It is recommended to demonstrate that the repositories policies and procedures (including any metadata standards, and costs involved) have been checked.

section.description: For example a data repository or archive
section.title: How will data for preservation be selected, and where data will be preserved long-term?

fieldSet.title: Indicate how the data will be shared

labelData.label: Couldn't find it? Insert it manually

fieldSet.title: Indicate whether potential users need specific tools to access and (re-)use the data
fieldSet.description: Consider the sustainability of software needed for accessing the data.

section.title: What methods or software tools are needed to access and use data?

fieldSet.title: Explain how the data might be re-used in other contexts
fieldSet.description: Persistent identifiers should be applied so that data can be reliably and efficiently located and referred to. Persistent identifiers also help to track citations and re-use.

fieldSet.title: Indicate whether a persistent identifier for the data will be pursued
fieldSet.description: Typically, a trustworthy, long-term repository will provide a persistent identifier.

section.description: Such as a Digital Object Identifier (DOI)
section.title: How will the application of a unique and persistent identifier to each data set be ensured?
Data Management Responsibilities And Resources

labelData.label: Couldn't find the name? Insert it manually

fieldSet.title: Outline the roles and responsibilities for data management/stewardship activities
fieldSet.description: Name responsible individual(s) where possible.
fieldSet.extendedDescription: For example data capture, metadata production, data quality, storage and backup, data archiving, and data sharing.

fieldSet.title: Is it a collaborative project?
Explain the co-ordination of data management responsibilities across partners
Indicate who is responsible for implementing the DMP, and for ensuring it is reviewed and, if necessary, revised
fieldSet.extendedDescription: Consider regular updates of the DMP

section.description: For example role, position, and institution. i.e. the data steward
section.title: Who will be responsible for data management?

fieldSet.title: Explain how the necessary resources (for example time) to prepare the data for sharing/preservation (data curation) have been costed in
fieldSet.description: Carefully consider and justify any resources needed to deliver the data. These may include storage costs, hardware, staff time, costs of preparing data for deposit, and repository charges.
fieldSet.extendedDescription: To understand what costs are allocated across the steps of a RDM lifecycle, how to estimate them and how to prevent them: https://www.openaire.eu/how-to-comply-to-h2020-mandates-rdm-costs

fieldSet.title: Indicate whether additional resources will be needed to prepare data for deposit or to meet any charges from data repositories
Explain how much is needed and how such costs will be covered

section.title: What resources will be dedicated to data management and ensuring that data will be FAIR (Findable, Accessible, Interoperable, Re-usable)?

fieldSet.comment: Data shared with access to the team

textListValues.textListValue: Text files - MS Word docs, .txt files, PDF, RTF, XML (Extensible Markup Language)
use of a secure data service

fieldSet.comment: One year after completion of project

field.textValue: Aggregated results will be presented in paper
Need for primary data according research plan
One person responsible for uploading raw data
We do not work with sensitive data

textListValues.textListValue: database with links to each item

field.textValue: Access to Onedrive will be maintained
